ctxMarket: Attention is focused on security across Solana DeFi after the $285 million Drift exploit. Despite recent breaches, there have been no widespread liquidity exits from Solana protocols.
ctxTalking:
• Solana Foundation’s new STRIDE security program and 24/7 threat monitoring for major DeFi protocols
• Stabble’s request for liquidity providers to withdraw funds after discovering a former employee’s North Korea ties
• Ongoing discussion about non-technical vulnerabilities, including espionage and insider threats
ctxMatters: Solana’s security overhaul signals a shift toward institutionalized protection, aiming to restore confidence after major exploits. Protocols with significant deposits may see increased scrutiny and external security support. The industry conversation is expanding beyond code to include human and organizational risks.
ctxSignal:
Signal: Solana Foundation’s STRIDE program and direct response to recent exploits; increased focus on insider and espionage risks.
Noise: Speculation about panic exits, which has not materialized according to current reports.
ctxGlossary:
STRIDE — Solana Foundation’s new ecosystem-wide security initiative
Liquidity Provider (LP) — An entity supplying assets to a DeFi protocol’s pool for trading or lending

readMore.title: Solana-based Stabble tells LPs to withdraw funds after identifying former North Korean employee
Solana Foundation to Help Secure DeFi Protocols Following $285 Million Drift Hack
